title: Senator Rosilicie Ochoa Bogh ( R-Yucaipa) has announced Senate Bill 270 passed out of the Senate Elections and Constitutional Amendments Committee with strong support. SB 270 updat

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 270 updates California’s recall process by protecting the personal information of voters who initiate a recall election.",
    "SB 270 ensures that the published Notice of Intention to Recall omits proponents’ signatures and street addresses.",
    "Publishing the personal information of voters can expose them to harassment, scams, and even violence.",
    "SB 270 strikes a balance between transparency and protecting those who engage in the democratic process.",
    "Existing law requires recall proponents to publish a notice containing their full name, signature, and home address in a newspaper of general circulation.",
    "The information required under existing law is also available electronically, creating risks of identity theft and doxxing.",
    "SB 270 requires that only the name, city, state, and ZIP code be published.",
    "Elections officials and the Secretary of State would be required to redact signatures and street addresses before releasing these records publicly.",
    "More personal information is stored on digital platforms, necessitating action to protect voters from unnecessary risks.",
    "SB 270 eliminates the unnecessary exposure of personally identifiable information in public records."
  ]
}
```
